es.name: Calculadora de Pladur
es.seo_title: Calculadora de Pladur – Planchas, Pasta y Tornillos
es.seo_description: Calcula cuántas planchas de pladur, kg de pasta y tornillos necesitas. Introduce las dimensiones de la pared y el tamaño de la plancha para obtener las cantidades...
es.description: Calculadora 1115. Free online calculator with formula, examples and step-by-step guide.
es.long_content:
¿Qué es la Calculadora de paneles de yeso?

Los paneles de yeso (placas de yeso) se venden en tamaños de láminas estándar (normalmente 1,2 m × 2,4 m) y cortar alrededor de puertas, ventanas y cajas eléctricas genera un desperdicio significativo. Esta calculadora divide el área total de paredes y techos por el tamaño de la hoja y agrega un factor de desperdicio del 8 % para brindarle el recuento exacto de hojas que debe ordenar.

También puede encontrar elRetaining Wall Calculator, Roofing Shingle Calculator, and Insulation Batt Calculator useful.

¿Quién usa esta calculadora?

Esta herramienta la utilizan los propietarios que agregan o reemplazan paneles de yeso, los constructores que enmarcan las paredes interiores y los contratistas de renovaciones que estiman los costos de los materiales.

Cómo utilizar la calculadora de paneles de yeso

  1. Ingresa tuLongitud de la pared.
  2. Ingresa tuAltura de la pared.
  3. Ingresa tuTamaño de hoja.
  4. Ingresa tuNúmero de capas.
  5. Click Calculatepara ver tus resultados al instante.

Formula

La calculadora utiliza la siguiente fórmula:

Area = Length × Height × layers; Sheets = ceil(area ÷ sheet_size × 1.08)

Ejemplo resuelto

Una habitación con 60 m² de paredes y techo utilizando láminas estándar de 1,2 × 2,4 m requiere 60 ÷ 2,88 = 20,8 láminas × 1,08 residuos =23 sheets.

Cálculo paso a paso

  1. Área de pared: 6 m × 2,7 m = 16,2 m²
  2. Hoja estándar (1,2 × 2,4 m) = 2,88 m²
  3. Hojas con 8% de desperdicio: techo(16,2 ÷ 2,88 × 1,08) = 7 hojas
  4. Compuesto para juntas: ~0,5 kg/m² = 8 kg; Cinta: ~1,5 m/m² = 24 m

Errores comunes que se deben evitar

  • Calcular solo el área neta sin agregar desperdicio para cortes alrededor de los puntos de venta
  • Olvidándose de los paneles de yeso del techo: utiliza el mismo tamaño de hoja pero necesita tornillos diferentes
  • No tener en cuenta los requisitos de firewall de doble capa en algunas habitaciones
Pro Tip:Cuelgue los paneles de yeso horizontalmente (el borde largo perpendicular a los montantes) para obtener paredes más fuertes con menos juntas a tope. Las juntas a tope son más difíciles de terminar que las juntas de borde cónico; minimícelas.

Preguntas frecuentes

¿Qué espesor de paneles de yeso debo usar?
12,5 mm (1/2 pulgada) es estándar para la mayoría de las paredes y techos interiores. Utilice 16 mm (5/8 de pulgada) para ensambles y techos resistentes al fuego con una separación entre vigas de 600 mm. Para paredes curvas se utiliza 9,5 mm.
¿Cómo calculo los paneles de yeso para un techo?
Calcule el área del techo por separado de las paredes. Para un techo plano, es simplemente largo × ancho. Los techos abovedados requieren calcular la superficie de pendiente utilizando el factor de inclinación (igual que para los techos).
¿Por qué agregar un factor de desperdicio del 8 % para los paneles de yeso?
Los cortes alrededor de puertas, ventanas, enchufes e interruptores crean recortes que a menudo son demasiado pequeños para reutilizarlos. El 8% es estándar para habitaciones rectangulares simples; Las habitaciones complejas con muchas aberturas pueden necesitar entre un 10% y un 12%.
¿Necesito paneles de yeso resistentes a la humedad en los baños?
Sí, use tablero verde (resistente a la humedad) o tablero de cemento detrás de las losas en áreas húmedas. Los paneles de yeso estándar absorben la humedad y se deteriorarán detrás de las baldosas. La mayoría de los códigos de construcción exigen paneles de cemento en los alrededores de duchas y bañeras.
¿Cuánto tiempo tarda el compuesto para paneles de yeso en secarse entre capas?
Cada capa de compuesto para juntas necesita 24 horas para secarse en condiciones normales (20°C, 50% de humedad). Acelere el secado con ventiladores y calor, o use un compuesto de fraguado que se endurezca químicamente en 20 a 45 minutos, independientemente de la humedad.
steps: Joint compound: ~0.5 kg/m² = 8 kg; Tape: ~1.5 m/m² = 24 m
mistakes: Calculating only the net area without adding waste for cuts around outlets
Forgetting ceiling drywall — it uses the same sheet size but needs different screws
Not accounting for double-layer firewall requirements in some rooms
es.formula_display: Area = Length × Height × layers; Sheets = ceil(area ÷ sheet_size × 1.08)

en.name: Drywall Calculator
en.seo_description: Calculate how many drywall sheets, kg of joint compound and screws you need. Enter wall dimensions and sheet size to get exact quantities instantly.
en.description: Free online drywall calculator. Enter wall length and wall height to get instant results.
en.long_content:
What is the Drywall Calculator?

Drywall (plasterboard) is sold in standard sheet sizes — most commonly 1.2 m × 2.4 m — and cutting around doors, windows, and electrical boxes creates significant waste. This calculator divides your total wall and ceiling area by sheet size and adds an 8% waste factor to give you the exact sheet count to order.

You may also find the Retaining Wall Calculator, Roofing Shingle Calculator, and Insulation Batt Calculator useful.

Who Uses This Calculator?

This tool is used by homeowners adding or replacing drywall, builders framing interior walls, and renovation contractors estimating material costs.

How to Use the Drywall Calculator

  1. Enter your Wall length.
  2. Enter your Wall height.
  3. Enter your Sheet size.
  4. Enter your Number of layers.
  5. Click Calculate to see your results instantly.

Formula

The calculator uses the following formula:

Area = Length × Height × layers; Sheets = ceil(area ÷ sheet_size × 1.08)

Worked Example

A room with 60 m² of walls and ceiling using standard 1.2 × 2.4 m sheets requires 60 ÷ 2.88 = 20.8 sheets × 1.08 waste = 23 sheets.

Step-by-Step Calculation

  1. Wall area: 6 m × 2.7 m = 16.2 m²
  2. Standard sheet (1.2 × 2.4 m) = 2.88 m²
  3. Sheets with 8% waste: ceil(16.2 ÷ 2.88 × 1.08) = 7 sheets
  4. Joint compound: ~0.5 kg/m² = 8 kg; Tape: ~1.5 m/m² = 24 m

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Calculating only the net area without adding waste for cuts around outlets
  • Forgetting ceiling drywall — it uses the same sheet size but needs different screws
  • Not accounting for double-layer firewall requirements in some rooms
Pro Tip: Hang drywall horizontally (long edge perpendicular to studs) for stronger walls with fewer butt joints. Butt joints are harder to finish than tapered edge joints — minimise them.

Frequently Asked Questions

What thickness drywall should I use?
12.5 mm (1/2 inch) is standard for most interior walls and ceilings. Use 16 mm (5/8 inch) for fire-rated assemblies and ceilings with 600 mm joist spacing. 9.5 mm is used for curved walls.

How do I calculate drywall for a ceiling?
Calculate ceiling area separately from walls. For a flat ceiling, it's simply length × width. Vaulted ceilings require calculating the slope surface area using the pitch factor (same as roofing).

Why add 8% waste factor for drywall?
Cuts around doors, windows, outlets, and switches create off-cuts that are often too small to reuse. 8% is standard for simple rectangular rooms; complex rooms with many openings may need 10–12%.

Do I need moisture-resistant drywall in bathrooms?
Yes — use green board (moisture-resistant) or cement board behind tile in wet areas. Standard drywall absorbs moisture and will deteriorate behind tiles. Cement board is required in shower and tub surrounds by most building codes.

How long does drywall compound take to dry between coats?
Each coat of joint compound needs 24 hours to dry in normal conditions (20°C, 50% humidity). Speed up drying with fans and heat, or use setting-type compound which chemically hardens in 20–45 minutes regardless of humidity.
en.desc: Free online drywall calculator. Enter wall length and wall height to get instant results.
steps: Joint compound: ~0.5 kg/m² = 8 kg; Tape: ~1.5 m/m² = 24 m
mistakes: Calculating only the net area without adding waste for cuts around outlets
Forgetting ceiling drywall — it uses the same sheet size but needs different screws
Not accounting for double-layer firewall requirements in some rooms
en.formula_display: Area = Length × Height × layers; Sheets = ceil(area ÷ sheet_size × 1.08)

What is the Drywall Calculator?

Drywall (plasterboard) is sold in standard sheet sizes — most commonly 1.2 m × 2.4 m — and cutting around doors, windows, and electrical boxes creates significant waste. This calculator divides your total wall and ceiling area by sheet size and adds an 8% waste factor to give you the exact sheet count to order.

You may also find the Retaining Wall Calculator, Roofing Shingle Calculator, and Insulation Batt Calculator useful.

Who Uses This Calculator?

This tool is used by homeowners adding or replacing drywall, builders framing interior walls, and renovation contractors estimating material costs.

How to Use the Drywall Calculator

  1. Enter your Wall length.
  2. Enter your Wall height.
  3. Enter your Sheet size.
  4. Enter your Number of layers.
  5. Click Calculate to see your results instantly.

Formula

The calculator uses the following formula:

Area = Length × Height × layers; Sheets = ceil(area ÷ sheet_size × 1.08)

Worked Example

A room with 60 m² of walls and ceiling using standard 1.2 × 2.4 m sheets requires 60 ÷ 2.88 = 20.8 sheets × 1.08 waste = 23 sheets.

Step-by-Step Calculation

  1. Wall area: 6 m × 2.7 m = 16.2 m²
  2. Standard sheet (1.2 × 2.4 m) = 2.88 m²
  3. Sheets with 8% waste: ceil(16.2 ÷ 2.88 × 1.08) = 7 sheets
  4. Joint compound: ~0.5 kg/m² = 8 kg; Tape: ~1.5 m/m² = 24 m

Häufige Fehler vermeiden

  • Calculating only the net area without adding waste for cuts around outlets
  • Forgetting ceiling drywall — it uses the same sheet size but needs different screws
  • Not accounting for double-layer firewall requirements in some rooms
Pro Tip: Hang drywall horizontally (long edge perpendicular to studs) for stronger walls with fewer butt joints. Butt joints are harder to finish than tapered edge joints — minimise them.

Häufig gestellte Fragen

What thickness drywall should I use?
12.5 mm (1/2 inch) is standard for most interior walls and ceilings. Use 16 mm (5/8 inch) for fire-rated assemblies and ceilings with 600 mm joist spacing. 9.5 mm is used for curved walls.

How do I calculate drywall for a ceiling?
Calculate ceiling area separately from walls. For a flat ceiling, it's simply length × width. Vaulted ceilings require calculating the slope surface area using the pitch factor (same as roofing).

Why add 8% waste factor for drywall?
Cuts around doors, windows, outlets, and switches create off-cuts that are often too small to reuse. 8% is standard for simple rectangular rooms; complex rooms with many openings may need 10–12%.

Do I need moisture-resistant drywall in bathrooms?
Yes — use green board (moisture-resistant) or cement board behind tile in wet areas. Standard drywall absorbs moisture and will deteriorate behind tiles. Cement board is required in shower and tub surrounds by most building codes.

How long does drywall compound take to dry between coats?
Each coat of joint compound needs 24 hours to dry in normal conditions (20°C, 50% humidity). Speed up drying with fans and heat, or use setting-type compound which chemically hardens in 20–45 minutes regardless of humidity.
